rulings.Case Title: J.R. MARKETING, LLC., A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al VS. HARTFORD CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 1, 2011, line 9, CROSS-DEFENDANTS J.R. MARKETING, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED, JANE RATTO, ROBERT RATTO, PENELOPE KANE, LENORE DEMARTINIS, GERMAIN DEMARTINIS, SQUIRE, SANDERS & DEMPSEY (US) LLP DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT. CROSS-DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION OF THE AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE INSUREDS. THERE IS NO AUTHORITY THAT HARTFORD MUST PROVIDE AN "ENTIRE DEFENSE" TO BE ENTITLED TO RELIEF. FURTHERMORE, THE CAUSE OF ACTION IS PLEADED WITH SUFFICIENT SPECIFICITY. THE DEMURRER TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION AS TO NON-INSUREDS IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE RIGHT TO REIMBURSEMENT ARISES FROM THE INSURER-INSURED RELATIONSHIP. BUSS V. SUP. CRT. (1997) 16 CAL.4TH 35. CROSS-DEFENDANTS DEMURRER TO THE 2ND AND 3RD CAUSES OF ACTION (UNJUST ENRICHMENT AND ACCOUNTING) IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. CROSS-PLAINTIFF'S REMEDY IS TO PURSUE THE REIMBURSEMENT CAUSE OF ACTION AGAINST THE INSUREDS. THE DEMURRER TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR RESCISSION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE NON-INSUREDS FOR THE SAME REASON PROVIDED ABOVE. THE DEMURRER ON THIS CAUSE OF ACTION AS TO THE INSUREDS IS OVERRULED. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: EDITH WONG VS. SUET YIM DIANA LAU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Suet Lau, Yun-Fai Lau Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 1, 2011, line 12, Defendants Suet Lau, Yun-Fai Lau Motion For Summary Adjudication. DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO WHETHER THE CONSIDERATION FOR THE PROMISSORY NOTE WAS EVER PAID. THE TERM "FOR VALUE RECEIVED" IN THE PROMISSORY NOTE IS AMBIGUOUS AND PAROLE EVIDENCE IS, THEREFORE, ADMISSIBLE TO ESTABLISH ITS MEANING. PLAINTIFF'S ARGUMENT THAT THE "VALUE RECEIVED" WAS IN FACT A CONTEMPORANEOUS ORAL PROMISE BY DEFENDANT DIANA LAU TO PLAINTIFF TO GIVE HER THE $300,000 IS A REASONABLE INTERPRETATION OF THE AMBIGUOUS TERM, THUS PRESENTING A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: UNITED BRANDS PRODUCTS DESIGN, DEVELOPMENT AND VS. MOSA INDUSTRIAL CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross-Complainant Mosa Industrial Corporation Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 1, 2011, line 13, Cross-Complainant Mosa Industrial Corporation Motion For Summary Judgment. Cross-Plaintiff¿s MSA/MSJ to the 1st cause of action (Breach of Contract) in the TAXC is denied. Cross-Pl. has argued that it duly performed under the contract by delivering good ordered by Cross-Def. However, as seen from the Court¿s analyses in the other motions, whether Cross-Plaintiff performed under the agreement is disputed. Cross-Plaintiff¿s MSA/MSJ to the 5th - 8th causes of action (Common Counts) in the TAXC is also denied. While Cross Plaintiff has shifted the burden on each of the common counts, Cross Defendant has successfully raised numerous triable issues of fact precluding MSA. For example, Cross-Def. raises triable issues of fact as to whether the price listed and charged on each invoice was accurate and whether at least on invoice was already paid. Also, there are triable issues of fact as to the amount of damages. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: UNITED BRANDS PRODUCTS DESIGN, DEVELOPMENT AND VS. MOSA INDUSTRIAL CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff United Brands Products Design, Development And Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 1, 2011, line 13, Plaintiff United Brands Products Design, Development And Motion For Summary Adjudication. Plaintiff¿s Motion for Summary Adjudication on the Breach of Contract cause of action is denied. Plaintiff claims that the Court can adjudicate the issue of duty and leave the issue of damages for later. While issues of duty may be adjudicated on a MSA/MSJ, it is limited to the existence and scope of a duty and does not extend to the breach of that duty. Plaintiff asks that Court to determine that Defendant breached a duty that was owed through it repudiation of the contract. This is beyond the permissible scope of a MSA on an issue of duty. Therefore, the MSA is denied. Plaintiff/Cross Defendant¿s Motion for Summary Adjudication on the 1st cause of action (Breach of Contract) in the Third Amended Cross-Complaint (TAXC) is denied. Cross Def. argues that Cross Pl. cannot recover for breach of contact because Cross-Pl. repudiated the contract and terminated the agreement. Cross-def. cites to Cross-Pl.¿s email to show that Cross Pl. repudiated the contract. However, both parties argue a different interpretation of what the email meant as well as other acts of Cross-Pl. This is enough to raise a triable issue of fact. The Court cannot grant MSA/MSJ based on inferences if contradicted by other inferences or evidence. Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 256. Cross-Def.¿s MSA on the 2nd cause of action (Breach of Implied Covenant) in the TAXC fails for the same reason. Cross-Def.¿s MSA on the 3rd cause of action (fraud/concealment) in the TAXC is granted. This cause of action asserts that Cross-Def. was under a duty to tell Cross-Pl. that Cross-Def. had initiated litigation against Cross-Pl. Cross-Def. argues that it was under no such duty and Cross-Pl. fails to address this issue in its opposition or present any relevant facts. Cross-Def.¿s MSA on the 4th cause of action (Fraud/false promises)

rulings.Case Title: UNITED BRANDS PRODUCTS DESIGN, DEVELOPMENT AND VS. MOSA INDUSTRIAL CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Mosa Industrial Corporation Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 1, 2011, line 13, Defendant Mosa Industrial Corporation Motion For Summary Judgment. Defendant¿s Motion for Summary Adjudication/ and Summary Judgment as to the breach of contract cause of action in the Second Amended Complaint is denied. While the Agreement is very poorly written, it appears that Plaintiff is correct that the quantity term appears clearly within its four corners ¿ ¿United shall ship minimum two containers in monthly¿. The analysis regarding breach/repudiation is the same as set forth in the tentative ruling on Plaintiff¿s MSA on the same issue. Defendant¿s argument that even if Plaintiff could prove repudiation, it did not actually cancel the Agreement is inapposite. Plaintiff dies not have to prove it canceled the Agreement in order to prevail on its breach of contract claim. Finally, on the alleged breach of the exclusivity provision of the Agreement, Plaintiff has likewise raised a triable issue of fact that Defendant sold product to Brown-Halco in North America through its subsidiary in violation of the Agreement. Plaintiff has not provided any evidence of damage on this claim, but only because Defendant has not yet provided the documents it was compelled to provide. =(302/LMG)
